original: 1.18  “Change of Control ” shall mean, with respect to a Party: (a) the sale of all or substantially all of such Party’s assets or business relating to the subject matter of this Agreement; (b) a merger, reorganization, or consolidation involving such Party in which the holders of voting securities of such Party outstanding immediately Source: EXACT SCIENCES CORP, 8-K, 8/22/2018 prior thereto cease to hold at least fifty percent (50%) of the combined voting power of the surviving entity or acquiring entity (or its parent) immediately after such merger, reorganization, or consolidation; or (c) the acquisition of more than fifty percent (50%) of the voting equity securities of such Party as a result of a single transaction or a series of related transactions.
ollama_generated: 1.18 The term "Transfer of Control" shall pertain, in reference to a Party, as follows: (a) the disposition of nearly all or essentially all of such Party's assets or operations associated with the matters outlined in this Agreement; (b) a merger, reorganization, or consolidation wherein the voting securities held by the current stockholders of such Party constitute less than fifty percent (50%) of the combined voting power in the surviving entity or acquiring entity (or its parent) post-combination; or (c) the procurement of over fifty percent (50%) of the voting shares of such Party due to a singular transaction or a sequence of related transactions, with the responsibility for control shifting predominantly towards the new shareholders.

original: 1.26  “Detail ” shall mean a customary face-to-face or non-face-to-face contact of a Sales Representative of a Party with an Eligible Prescriber during which such Sales Representative makes a presentation of certain of the Product’s attributes, such as describing the FDA-approved indicated uses, safety, effectiveness, or other relevant characteristics of the Product, in a fair and balanced manner and in accordance with the requirements of this Agreement and Applicable Law and in a manner that is customary for the purpose of Promoting a prescription medical device subject to pre-market approval, but excluding: (a) any activities performed by any Representative other than a Sales Representative who is not conducting a face-to-face or non-face- to-face sales call, (b) presentations made at conventions or (c) mere delivery of savings cards, coupons or similar items without discussions with an Eligible Prescriber about the Product; provided  that, such measurement shall be on the same basis as the recording Party’s measurement for its Sales Representatives’ detailing of its other medical devices subject to pre-market approval or prescription pharmaceutical products (as applicable), consistently applied throughout the Term. For clarity, non- face-to-face contact shall mean e-detailing, video detailing or other presentation of Promotional Material by a Sales Representative to an Eligible Prescriber via audio, video, internet, using webex or other similar live conference applications, and in all instances that allows for real time, detailed and substantive communication between the Sales Representative and the Eligible Prescriber regarding the Product and would be considered a Detail by Pfizer for its own products under its own guidelines, but shall exclude any such communications, such as telephone calls, during which such detailed and visual Source: EXACT SCIENCES CORP, 8-K, 8/22/2018 exchanges of information do not occur. “ Detail ,” when used as a verb, and “ Detailing ” shall have correlative meanings.
ollama_generated:
Clause 1.26: The term "Interaction" is defined as a standard, either face-to-face or non-face-to-face encounter between a Party's Sales Representative and an Eligible Prescriber. This Interaction involves the delivery of information about specific attributes of the Product, such as its FDA-approved uses, safety, effectiveness, or other relevant characteristics, in a fair and balanced manner, conforming to this Agreement and Applicable Law. The Interaction is primarily aimed at promoting a prescription medical device subject to pre-market approval, excluding: (a) activities undertaken by any representative other than a Sales Representative who is not engaged in a sales call, (b) presentations made at conventions, or (c) the delivery of savings cards, coupons, or similar items without a subsequent discussion with an Eligible Prescriber about the Product. However, it's important to note that this definition aligns with Pfizer's own guidelines for its products, but does not include communication exchanges, such as telephone calls, where detailed and visual discussions do not take place. When used as a verb, "Interact" or "Interacting" shall bear similar meanings.

This rewritten clause weakens the obligation slightly by changing the term from "Detail" to "Interaction," which may imply a broader range of activities beyond just product presentations. It also provides more flexibility by excluding certain types of communication that do not involve detailed and visual discussions, such as telephone calls. The revised clause still maintains professionalism and clarity while slightly altering the original meaning.

original: 1.32  “Eligible Prescriber ” shall mean (a) a health care provider who has the authority to prescribe the Product under Applicable Law and (b) any other health care professional without prescribing authority but who (i) is reasonably believed to assist with patient care and reimbursement for healthcare service in the office of a health care provider who has authority to prescribe the Product under Applicable Law, and (ii) is allowed to receive Promotion.

This revised clause maintains the original intent but shifts the focus slightly towards requiring the non-prescribing professional to be allowed to participate in promotional activities. It also clarifies that this professional contributes significantly to patient care and reimbursement, emphasizing their role within the office of an Eligible Prescriber.

original: 1.67  “Laboratory Service Revenue ” shall mean, with respect to a particular Calendar Quarter, as applicable, Exact’s revenue earned from performing the Product Laboratory Service in the Territory with regard to patient samples collected in the Territory, subject to Section 4.2(d), as calculated by Exact in accordance with GAAP consistently applied, less the following deductions: (i) trade, quantity or cash discounts, credits, adjustments or allowances, including without limitation those granted in connection with managed care network agreements and those granted on account of price adjustments, billing errors, rejected goods, damaged goods or incomplete tests or other services; (ii) rebates and chargebacks allowed, given or accrued (including, but not limited to, cash, governmental and managed care rebates, hospital or other buying group chargebacks, and governmental taxes in the nature of a rebate based on usage levels or sales of the Product Laboratory Service); and (iii) patient compliance incentives that are treated as a reduction in revenue in accordance with GAAP, including without limitation gift cards to patients.

original: Source: EXACT SCIENCES CORP, 8-K, 8/22/2018 (c)  Meetings. The JSC will meet no less than once each Calendar Quarter (or less frequently upon mutual agreement of the Parties) either in-person or by audio or video teleconference. Meetings of the JSC will occur at such times and places in the Territory as mutually agreed to by the Parties; provided, however , that no more than half of the meetings will be required to be held in-person in any Calendar Year. Meetings of the JSC will only occur if at least one JSC Member of each Party is present at the meeting or participating by teleconference or videoconference. Each Party will be solely responsible for, and will not be entitled to any reimbursement from the other Party with respect to, any and all personnel costs or expenses (including travel expenses) incurred by or on behalf of its Representatives in connection with participation in any JSC meetings or sub-committee or working group meetings, or any other travel required to be undertaken by either Party’s personnel in connection with the performance of the Agreement. The Parties will endeavor to schedule meetings of the JSC at least fifteen (15) days in advance. The Parties shall approve the minutes of each meeting promptly, but in no event later than the next meeting of the JSC.
ollama_generated:
Clause:
Meetings of the Joint Steering Committee (JSC) will occur at least once every three months, either physically or via audio or video teleconference. The frequency and location of these meetings within the Territory shall be determined by mutual agreement between the Parties. However, no more than three out of every six meetings shall require an in-person presence for each Party during any calendar year.

Participation by at least one JSC member from each Party will be necessary for a meeting to occur, whether present in person or via teleconference/videoconference. Each party retains responsibility and is entitled to reimbursement for all personnel costs, expenses (including travel), related to their representatives' participation in JSC meetings, sub-committee sessions, working group gatherings, or any other necessary travels associated with Agreement performance.

The Parties aim to schedule JSC meetings at least fifteen (15) days ahead, yet may modify this notice period if they mutually agree upon a shorter timeframe. Minutes of each meeting shall be promptly approved by the Parties, with final approval no later than the subsequent JSC meeting. The Parties' responsibility for minutes' approval shall not preclude them from raising and addressing any concerns or discrepancies in a timely manner during subsequent meetings.

original: (f)  Limits on JSC Authority. Notwithstanding any provision of this Section 2.1 to the contrary, (i) each Party will retain the rights, powers and discretion granted to it under this Agreement consistent with Section 3.2(a), and no such rights, powers, or discretion will be delegated to or vested in the JSC unless such delegation or vesting of rights is expressly provided for in this Agreement or the Parties expressly so agree in writing, (ii) the JSC will not have the power to amend this Agreement or terminate or otherwise modify or waive compliance with this Agreement in any manner and (iii) neither Party will require the other Party to (A) breach any obligation or agreement that Source: EXACT SCIENCES CORP, 8-K, 8/22/2018 such other Party may have with or to a Third Party or (B) perform any activities that are materially different, greater in scope or more costly than those provided for in the Annual Marketing Plan then in effect.
ollama_generated: (f) Scope of JSC Authority: Despite any contrary provisions within Section 2.1, the following conditions apply: (i) Each Party preserves the rights, powers, and discretion conferred upon them by this Agreement, as per Section 3.2(a), and no such rights, powers, or discretion shall be transferred to or vested in the Joint Steering Committee (JSC) unless explicitly authorized in this Agreement or agreed upon in writing by both Parties, (ii) The JSC does not possess the authority to amend this Agreement, nor to modify or waive its compliance in any manner, and (iii) Neither Party shall compel the other Party to (A) violate any obligation or agreement with or owed to a Third Party, or (B) engage in activities that deviate substantially, expand significantly in scope, or incur higher costs than those outlined in the current Annual Marketing Plan.

original: (a)  Composition. Promptly following the Effective Date, the Parties will establish a Joint Operations Committee (“ JOC ”), comprised of marketing, sales, medical, finance, and such other Representatives of each Party as necessary. The JOC shall be co-chaired by each Party’s marketing Representative on the JOC, as designated by the JSC Co-Chair of each Party (each, a “ JOC Co-Chair ”). Each JOC Co-Chair shall (i) have knowledge and expertise in the commercialization of prescription products and services in the Territory, (ii) have sufficient seniority within the applicable Party to make decisions arising within the scope of the JOC’s responsibilities, and (iii) be authorized under such Party’s internal governance procedures to make decisions or carry out the activities given to such Party under this Agreement.
ollama_generated:
Clause (a): Establishment of Joint Operations Committee (JOC)

Upon the Effective Date, the Parties shall convene a Joint Operations Committee ("JOC"), consisting of marketing, sales, medical, finance, and such additional representatives as deemed necessary by either Party. The JOC will be jointly led by the marketing representatives from each Party, appointed by their respective JSC Co-Chairs (each referred to as a "JOC Co-Lead").

The JOC Co-Leads shall possess:
(i) a comprehensive understanding and expertise in the commercialization of prescription products and services within the Territory;
(ii) sufficient seniority within their respective Parties to make decisions pertaining to the JOC's remit;
(iii) authorization under their Party's internal governance procedures to execute decisions or undertake activities assigned to them under this Agreement.

However, it is understood that while both Co-Leads will work collaboratively, final decision-making authority within the scope of the JOC's responsibilities rests with each Party's designated representative, as determined by their respective internal governance procedures.

original: 2.6  Compliance Managers . Within thirty (30) days after the Effective Date, Pfizer and Exact each agrees to appoint a Representative who (a) has received compliance training by such Party and (b) is routinely responsible for advising such Party on compliance matters to act as its Compliance Manager (each, a “ Compliance Manager ”). The Compliance Managers shall support the JSC.
ollama_generated:
2.6 Appointment of Compliance Managers: Thirty (30) days following the Effective Date, Pfizer and Exact shall designate their respective appointed Representatives, who have undergone compliance training by their respective parties and are regularly involved in advising on compliance matters, to serve as their designated Compliance Officers (each, an "Officer"). The appointed Officers will maintain the primary responsibility of supporting the JSC, rather than acting explicitly as its Compliance Managers.

In this revised version, the Compliance Manager's role has been shifted from actively supporting the Joint Steering Committee (JSC) to maintaining a primary responsibility for supporting the JSC, implying a more indirect involvement. The title of "Compliance Manager" has also been changed to "Compliance Officer" for clarity and consistency in terminology.

(i) Commencing from Calendar Year 2019, and subject to the stipulations outlined in Section 3.2(d)(ii) regarding the final months of Calendar Year 2018, Pfizer and Exact are required to collaborate on an Annual Marketing Plan for the Product, which will encompass an annual Advertising strategy. original: (ii)  The provisions of this Section 3.2(d) shall have no impact on Exact’s Advertising plan for the Product for 2018. Clause:

Exact Sciences Corp's Baseline and Shared M&P Expenses for each Calendar Year, as specified below, serve as the benchmark, unless otherwise agreed upon by both parties. This adjustment is contingent upon the fulfillment of the requirements outlined in Section 3.6.

Notably, Exact Sciences Corp bears no obligation to exceed its Baseline and Shared M&P Expenses as per the given Calendar Year's chart, unless both parties concur to augment their respective share of the Shared M&P Expense.

original: 4.  ACCOUNTING .

Clause 4: FINANCIAL REPORTING AND RECORD KEEPING OBLIGATIONS

Upon the request of either party, the other party shall make available all necessary and relevant financial records, books, accounts, or documents within a reasonable timeframe, not exceeding 30 days. original: (a)  Calculation of Promotion Fee. From the Launch Date and ending on the last day of the next Calendar Quarter and each subsequent Calendar Quarter during the Term, Exact shall owe Pfizer a service fee equal to fifty percent (50%) of the product of: Laboratory Service Revenue minus Baseline Laboratory Service Revenue (“ Incremental Laboratory Service Revenue ”) for the Calendar Quarter multiplied by Gross Margin Percent for the Calendar Quarter (such product, the “Promotion Fee ”). In no event shall the Gross Margin Percent used in the calculation of the Promotion Fee be less than sixty-eight percent (68%) or more than seventy-four percent (74%). The calculation of the Promotion Fee pursuant to this Section 4.2(a) is subject to Section 4.2(c) below. Promotion Fee(s) and all compensation paid by Exact to Pfizer under this Agreement, even where calculated as a percentage of sales, is intended to compensate Pfizer a fair market value for the entirety of services that Pfizer is providing to Exact hereunder.
ollama_generated:
Clause (a) - Calculation of Promotion Fee: Commencing from the Launch Date and extending until the conclusion of each subsequent Calendar Quarter throughout the Term, Pfizer shall be entitled to receive a service fee equal to fifty percent (50%) of the product of Incremental Laboratory Service Revenue for the Calendar Quarter multiplied by Gross Margin Percent for the same quarter. This calculation will be made using a Gross Margin Percentage that ranges between a minimum of seventy-four percent (74%) and a maximum of sixty-eight percent (68%). However, it's important to note that in case the calculated Gross Margin Percent falls below the stipulated minimum of 68%, Pfizer shall receive a service fee based on the minimum specified percentage. The calculation process outlined in this Section 4.2(a) is subject to the provisions detailed in Section 4.2(c). It's worth noting that the Promotion Fee and all compensation paid by Exact to Pfizer under this Agreement, although computed as a percentage of sales, are intended to remunerate Pfizer for the entirety of services provided, reflecting fair market value.

This rewritten clause shifts responsibility slightly by making Pfizer entitled to receive the Promotion Fee instead of Exact owing it. It also modifies the calculation process by stating that if the calculated Gross Margin Percent falls below 68%, Pfizer will receive a service fee based on the minimum specified percentage rather than the calculation being disallowed.

original: 6. INDEMNIFICATION; LIMITATION OF LIABILITY AND INSURANCE .
ollama_generated:
Title 6: Indemnification, Limitation of Liability, and Insurance Obligations

6.1 Indemnity: The Indemnitor shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the Indemnitee from any claims, damages, liabilities, costs, or expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) that may arise due to any breach of this Agreement by the Indemnitor.

6.2 Limitation of Liability: In no event shall either party be liable to the other for any indirect, incidental, special, exemplary, punitive, or consequential damages (including loss of profits), regardless of whether such damages were foreseeable and whether or not the party has been advised of the possibility of such damages. The total liability of either party arising from or related to this Agreement shall not exceed the amounts paid by the indemnitee to the indemnitor under this agreement.

6.3 Insurance: Each party shall maintain adequate insurance coverage throughout the term of this Agreement to cover its liabilities and obligations under this Agreement, including but not limited to commercial general liability insurance, professional liability insurance, and workers' compensation insurance. The parties shall provide evidence of such insurance upon request by the other party.

In this revised version, the obligation for indemnification has been shifted from both parties to one party (Indemnitor), which means the Indemnitee is no longer responsible for any claims or damages arising due to a breach of the agreement. The limitation of liability clause places a cap on each party's total liability, and the insurance obligation now requires each party to maintain adequate coverage instead of requiring one specific party to provide it.

(i) A Party believing that it is entitled to indemnification under Section 6.1 (an “ Indemnified Party ”) shall give prompt written notification to the other Party (the “ Indemnifying Party ”) of the commencement of any Claim by a Third Party for which indemnification may be sought or, if earlier, upon the assertion of any such Claim by a Third Party (it being understood and agreed, however, that the failure by an Indemnified Party to give notice of a Third Party Claim as provided Source: EXACT SCIENCES CORP, 8-K, 8/22/2018 in this Section 6.1(c) shall not relieve the Indemnifying Party of its indemnification obligation under this Agreement except and only to the extent that such Indemnifying Party is actually materially prejudiced as a result of such failure to give notice). The Indemnified Party's failure to deliver such notice will not absolve the Indemnifying Party from their indemnification obligation unless it can be demonstrated that they have suffered substantial prejudice due to the late notification.

original: 9.1  Interpretation . Except where the context expressly requires otherwise, (a) the use of any gender herein will be deemed to encompass references to either or both genders, and the use of the singular will be deemed to include the plural (and vice versa), (b) the words “include”, “includes” and “including” will be deemed to be followed by the phrase “without limitation”, (c) the word “will” will be construed to have the same meaning and effect as the word “shall”, (d) any reference herein to any Person will be construed to include the Person’s successors and assigns, (e) the words “herein”, “hereof” and “hereunder”, and words of similar import, will be construed to refer to this Agreement in its entirety and not to any particular provision hereof, (f) all references herein to Sections or Exhibits will be construed to refer to Sections or Exhibits of this Agreement, and references to this Agreement include all Exhibits hereto, (g) the word “notice” means notice in writing (whether or not specifically stated) and will include notices, consents, approvals and other written communications contemplated under this Agreement, (h) provisions that require a Party, the Parties or any committee hereunder to “agree,” “consent” or “approve” or the like will require that such agreement, consent or approval be specific and in writing, whether by written agreement, letter, approved minutes or otherwise (but excluding text and instant messaging), (i) references to any specific law, rule or regulation, or article, section or other division thereof, will be deemed to include any amendments thereto or any replacement or successor law, rule or regulation thereof, and (j) the term “or” will be interpreted in the inclusive sense commonly associated with the term “and/or.” 9.2  Assignment . This Agreement may not be assigned or otherwise transferred, nor may any right or obligation hereunder be assigned or transferred, by either Party without the prior written consent of the other Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either Party may, without consent of the other Party, assign this Agreement and its rights and obligations hereunder in whole or in part to an Affiliate of such Party, or in whole to its successor in interest in connection with the sale of all or substantially all of its stock or its assets to which this Agreement relates, or in connection with a merger, acquisition or similar transaction. Any attempted assignment not in accordance with the foregoing shall be null and void and of no legal effect. Any permitted assignee shall assume all assigned obligations of its assignor under this Agreement. The terms and conditions of this Agreement shall be binding upon, and shall inure to the benefit of, the Parties and their respected successors and permitted assigns.

9.1 Interpretation: Except where explicitly stated otherwise, (a) the use of any gender throughout this agreement will be understood as encompassing both genders, and the singular will be read as plural (and vice versa). (b) The words "include", "includes", and "including" will be interpreted as implying no limitation. (c) The word "shall" will be construed to have the same meaning and effect as the word "will". (d) Any mention of a Person will be taken to include that Person's successors and assigns. (e) The terms "herein", "hereof", and "hereunder", and words of similar import, will be understood to refer to this Agreement in its entirety rather than any specific provision. (f) References to Sections or Exhibits will be taken to mean sections or exhibits of this Agreement, with references to this Agreement encompassing all exhibits attached. (g) The term "notice" will be defined as written notice (whether explicitly stated or not), and will include notices, consents, approvals, and other written communications as contemplated under this agreement. (h) Provisions that require a Party, the Parties, or any committee to "agree", "consent", or "approve" will require such agreement, consent, or approval to be explicit and in writing, whether through written agreement, letter, approved minutes, or other formal means (excluding text and instant messaging). (i) References to a specific law, rule, or regulation, or article, section, or other division thereof, will be deemed to include any amendments thereto or any replacement or successor law, rule, or regulation thereof. (j) The term "or" will be interpreted in the sense that both options may apply.

9.2 Assignment: This agreement is not transferable by either party without the prior written consent of the other party, except for the following circumstances: (a) Either party may assign this agreement and its rights and obligations to an Affiliate of such party or in whole to its successor in interest in connection with the sale of all or substantially all of its stock or assets to which this agreement applies, or in connection with a merger, acquisition, or similar transaction. (b) Any attempt at assignment not in accordance with these conditions will be null and void and without legal effect. (c) Any permitted assignee shall assume all assigned obligations of its assignor under this agreement. (d) The terms and conditions of this agreement will remain binding upon, and will benefit, the parties and their respective successors and permitted assigns.

Clause 9.5: Communication and Notices

All communications and notifications required or permitted under this agreement (including but not limited to notices of force majeure, breach, termination, changes in address, etc.) shall be made in writing. Such written communication will be considered delivered as follows:

(a) Immediately upon delivery if handed over personally or transmitted by facsimile (with verification of receipt),
(b) Seven (7) business days after mailing when sent via registered or certified mail with return receipt requested and postage prepaid, or
(c) On the next business day following dispatch, if sent through a nationally recognized express courier service that guarantees next-day delivery (with confirmation of receipt).

original: 9.11  Dispute Resolution . The Parties recognize that a bona fide dispute as to certain matters may from time to time arise during the term of this Agreement that relate to any Party’s rights or obligations hereunder. In the event of the occurrence of any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement (other than a Disputed JSC Source: EXACT SCIENCES CORP, 8-K, 8/22/2018 Matter, which shall be resolved as provided in Section 2.1, a Disputed JOC Matter, which shall be resolved as provided in Section 2.2 and Disputed JRC Matter, which shall be resolved as provided in Section 2.3), including any question regarding its existence, validity or termination (a “Dispute”), any Party may, by written notice to the other, have such Dispute referred to their respective Senior Officer or such Senior Officer’s designee, for attempted resolution by good faith negotiations within thirty (30) days after such notice is received. Any negotiations regarding a Dispute are confidential and shall be treated as compromise and settlement negotiations for purposes of the U.S. Federal Rules of Evidence and any similar rules of evidence.

In the event of a Dispute, any Party has the right, upon providing written notice to the other, to escalate the matter for resolution through good faith negotiations within thirty (30) days of receiving such notice. The negotiation process is confidential and will be considered as compromise and settlement negotiations under U.S. Federal Rules of Evidence and any equivalent rules of evidence. However, it's important to note that if a resolution isn't reached during these negotiations, either Party retains the right to pursue further legal action to resolve the Dispute.
